items.title: Sources: Barça must pay Man Utd full Rashford fee
items.summary: Manchester United have no plans to re-negotiate the terms of Marcus Rashford's potential permanent move to Barcelona, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Arteta suggests warmup change amid injuries
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed Arsenal are assessing their warm-up routines after four players suffered fitness scares just before matches.

items.title: Mexico confirms trio of World Cup warm-up games
items.summary: Mexico's men's national team will finish its preparations for the World Cup with warm-up matches against Ghana, Australia, and Serbia.

items.title: Inter's Bastoni sorry for celebrating Juve red card
items.summary: Inter Milan defender Alessandro Bastoni apologised for celebrating the controversial sending off of Juventus defender Pierre Kalulu's in Saturday's Derby d'Italia.

items.title: VI Live: NEC dankzij Ogawa weer naast Sparta, PSG mist strafschop
items.summary: Sparta Rotterdam en NEC maken dinsdagavond hun door de sneeuw geteisterde duel van afgelopen weekend in. Daarnaast gaat de tussenronde van de Champions League van start, met onder meer Real Madrid, Paris Saint-Germain, Borussia Dortmund en Juventus. Volg het in de VI Live.

items.title: Benfica v Real Madrid, Monaco v PSG, and more: Champions League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League updates from the first-leg matches ⚽ Live scores | Follow us over on Bluesky | And email Scott The free kick is just to the right of the Juve box. Gabriel Sara whips it in viciously. All Davinson Sánchez has to do is tickle the ball with his eyebrow to send it across Michele Di Gregorio and into the top left. He couldn’t miss! Sara put that on a plate, a world-class delivery. And the hosts respond to Juve’s turnaround with one of their own. What a match this is! Galatasaray 2-2 Juventus. Juan Cabal brings down Barış Alper Yılmaz, flying down the right, just to the side of the box. Cabal goes into the book, and nearly earns a second booking by pawing at the referee’s arm, Danny Makkelie telling him to get his hands off in angry fashion. And from the resulting free kick … Continue reading...

items.title: Feyenoord training moved to Belgium for Sterling
items.summary: Feyenoord will train in Belgium this week instead of their home in Rotterdam so new signing Raheem Sterling can work with his new team-mates.

items.title: Champions League live updates: Benfica host Real Madrid, Monaco host PSG
items.summary: Enjoy all the updates from today's knockout phase playoff first legs, including Benfica vs. Real Madrid and AS Monaco vs. Paris Saint-Germain.

items.title: Lang steelt heldenrol Koopmeiners in zeer vermakelijk Champions League-duel
items.summary: Galatasaray heeft dinsdagavond enorm goede zaken gedaan in de Champions League. Teun Koopmeiners leek eindelijk een heldenrol bij Juventus te pakken, maar Noa Lang stal deze van zijn landgenoot. De luide fans in Istanbul waren getuige van een zeer vermakelijk duel met zeven goals in het miljardenbal.

items.title: Dit kan AZ verwachten van FC Noah
items.summary: In de tussenronde van de Conference League treft AZ een grote onbekende. Geen toeval, want FC Noah bestaat pas een decennium als profclub. De huidige nummer vijf van de Armeense competitie heeft voor een flinke stunt gezorgd, door Europees te overleven. Analyse van een opponent die een klassieke vreemdelingenlegioen-tactiek hanteert.

items.title: Koopmeiners grijpt zijn moment en maakt eerste Champions League-goals ooit
items.summary: Teun Koopmeiners heeft dinsdagavond een mijlpaal bereikt in de Champions League. De 27-jarige Nederlander maakte niet één, maar twee doelpunten in het miljardenbal. En dat terwijl hij nog nooit had gescoord in de Champions League.

items.title: Sneijder bekritiseert Champions League-optreden Makkelie: 'Absoluut rood'
items.summary: Wesley Sneijder is met stomheid geslagen na de eerste helft tussen Galatasaray en Juventus in de tussenronde van de Champions League. Volgens de recordinternational had Danny Makkelie een rode kaart moeten geven aan Weston McKennie, die volgens hem een slaande beweging maakte naar Roland Sallai.

items.title: Arsenal announce rise in season-ticket prices for fifth consecutive season
items.summary: Arsenal Supporters’ Trust ‘disappointed’ by increase Club also confirm safe standing at Clock End next year Arsenal have announced that season-ticket prices will rise for a fifth consecutive season, with the cost of a ticket for the most dedicated fans rising by an average of 3.9%. The Premier League leaders, who face Wolves at Molineux on Wednesday, say they have made the decision as part of a continued push towards “financial stability” and confirmed that safe standing would be introduced to the Clock End next season, after consultation with supporters. Continue reading...

items.title: How key F1 meetings could have huge implications on 2026 season
items.summary: The final test of the 2026 Formula 1 pre-season gets under way on Wednesday, but the most crucial action of the day may well be away from the track at the Bahrain International Circuit.

items.title: Raya talks Arsenal's bid to win silverware and squad togetherness
items.summary: David Raya discusses his side's bid to win silverware this season and togetherness in the Gunners squad

items.title: Castleford 'heartbroken' as star signing suffers season-ending ACL injury
Australian player claims tennis is 'racist' and 'misogynistic' as she quits the sport
items.summary: Australia's Destanee Aiava has announced her retirement from tennis at the age of 25 and claims she is turning her back on the game's "racist, misogynistic, homophobic and hostile" culture.

items.title: Football Daily | Refereeing is stuck in a frustrating feedback loop, with no easy way out
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! It’s been another rough few days for Europe’s referees. On Saturday, Chris Kavanagh and his assistants had a night to forget at Villa Park without the comfort blanket of VAR. One poor decision is forgivable, two is careless, but there were a hat-trick of howlers, with Tammy Abraham clearly offside for Aston Villa’s opener. Lucas Digne needed his own dedicated review booth, escaping with a yellow card for an agricultural tackle on Jacob Murphy and avoiding a penalty for handball. Officials ruled that Digne handled the ball outside the penalty area, an impressive feat given that he both took off and landed inside the box as he jumped to block a cross. Kavanagh and one of his assistants have been stood down by PGMO, presumably rebranded because they can’t stop dropping Ls. Continue reading...

items.title: Igor Tudor hopes courage and confidence can arrest Tottenham slide
items.summary: Interim manager: ‘There is no time to find excuses’ ‘The position of the club is one that nobody can accept’ Igor Tudor says there is “no time to find excuses” as he attempts to halt Tottenham’s Premier League slide. The 47-year-old former Juventus manager, brought in last week as the sacked Thomas Frank’s replacement until the end of the season, believes instilling the players with confidence is his most important task. “The team need, I believe, first of all, to get some confidence, to get some courage, but also, in same way, the concrete things in the pitch,” Tudor told Spursplay. “Of course, I’m coming here knowing that situation is not easy. There is no time to find excuses. What I said from the first day here, each of us need to give something more, something extra.” Continue reading...

items.title: Vítor Pereira insists he shares trust with ‘emotional’ Evangelos Marinakis
items.summary: ‘He trusts my work, I trust his personality’ Coach and owner have worked together at Olympiakos Vítor Pereira believes the trust he has with Nottingham Forest’s owner, Evangelos Marinakis, means he can survive the poisoned chalice of the City Ground job. The Portuguese has become Forest’s fourth manager of the season after Sean Dyche followed Nuno Espírito Santo and Ange Postecoglou out of the door as Marinakis acted in an attempt to avoid Premier League relegation. Continue reading...
